scope.summary: Implement all five GraphicBlendMode variants (Normal, Multiply, Screen, Overlay, Emboss) in the wgpu PBR fragment shader and validate each blend mode produces the correct compositing result against the base fabric texture
acceptance_criteria: The pbr_cloth.wgsl fragment shader implements the five GraphicBlendMode variants: Normal (standard alpha-over), Multiply (darkening), Screen (brightening), Overlay (contrast), Emboss (additive normal map contribution)
The Emboss blend mode adds the graphic layer image as a normal map contribution to the existing normal map rather than to the color channels
The blend mode is read from the per-graphic-layer GPU buffer at render time (not a compile-time specialization)
A model calling tailor_graphic_layer_add can specify any of the five blend modes and observe the correct result in a subsequent tailor_capture_frame call
Visual integration test: place a dark graphic on a white cotton panel with Multiply blend; captured solid render shows the graphic at reduced luminance over the fabric
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture matrix with all five blend modes on the same graphic layer; assert visually distinguishable results per blend mode
scope.risk_if_missed: Without correct blend mode implementation, all graphic layers composite as flat Normal alpha-over. Screen-printed looks (Multiply), foil effects (Screen), embroidery (Emboss) all fail. Production garments cannot be textured for their target market look.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
